shots.title: Option 1 — Character B Speaks To Friend
shots.note: Camera B alternative: both bikes face the same direction; brunette listener shoulder sits blurred on the right edge while blonde speaker turns sideways toward her friend.

shots.title: Option 2 — Character A Reacts To Friend
shots.note: Camera A alternative: both bikes face the same direction; brunette speaker is framed on the right while blonde listener shoulder sits blurred in the bottom-left foreground.

shots.title: Option 3 — Candid Side Observer
shots.note: A wider observer-style angle with both bikes parallel and facing forward. Best for establishing or silent transition coverage.

shots.title: Option 4 — Tight Character B Crop
shots.note: Tighter Camera B crop: brunette shoulder blurred on the right, blonde speaker close enough for lip sync, still facing her friend.

shots.title: Option 5 — Tight Character A Crop
shots.note: Tighter Camera A crop: blonde shoulder blurred bottom-left, brunette framed on the right, still talking sideways to her friend.

shots.title: Option 6 — Between-Bikes Observer Crop
shots.note: Soft observer crop from between the bikes. Best as a candid setup or non-dialogue transition because both women are visible.

shots.title: CTA Direction — Website Screenshot With Voiceover
shots.note: Recommended CTA treatment: keep the real screenshot as the visual and place Character B's CTA audio over it, instead of showing her performing the CTA to camera.
